questions.question_text: Pick out the line that suggests that the child is afraid of snakes. [Garden Snake]
questions.question_html: Pick out the line that suggests that the child is afraid of snakes. [Garden Snake]

solutions.text: The line that suggests that the child is afraid of snakes is 'I saw a snake and ran away...'
solutions.html: The line that suggests that the child is afraid of snakes is 'I saw a snake and ran away...'

questions.question_text: Which line shows a complete change of the child's attitude towards snakes? Read it aloud.
questions.question_html: Which line shows a complete change of the child’s attitude towards snakes? Read it aloud.

solutions.text: The line that shows a change in the child's attitude is - 'I'll stand aside and watch him pass, And tell myself, "There's no mistake, It's just a harmless garden snake!"'
solutions.html: The line that shows a change in the child's attitude is - 'I’ll stand aside and watch him pass, And tell myself, “There’s no mistake, It’s just a harmless garden snake!”'

questions.question_text: But mother says that kind is good... What is the mother referring to? Answer in the context of Garden Snake.
questions.question_html: But mother says that kind is good... What is the mother referring to? Answer in the context of Garden Snake.

solutions.text: The mother is referring to the garden snake, saying it is the harmless kind of snake.
solutions.html: The mother is referring to the garden snake, saying it is the harmless kind of snake.

questions.question_text: Find the word that refers to the snake's movements in the grass. Answer in the context of Garden Snake.
questions.question_html: Find the word that refers to the snake’s movements in the grass. Answer in the context of Garden Snake.

solutions.text: 'Wiggles' is the word that refers to the snake's movements in the grass.
solutions.html: 'Wiggles' is the word that refers to the snake’s movements in the grass.

questions.question_text: There are four pairs of rhyming words in the poem. Say them aloud. Answer in the context of Garden Snake.
questions.question_html: There are four pairs of rhyming words in the poem. Say them aloud. Answer in the context of Garden Snake.

solutions.text:
The four pairs are:
away - say
good - food
grass - pass
mistake - snake
solutions.html:
The four pairs are:

  1. away - say
  2. good - food
  3. grass - pass
  4. mistake - snake

questions.question_text: A snake has no legs or feet, but it moves very fast. Can you guess how? Discuss in the group.
questions.question_html: A snake has no legs or feet, but it moves very fast. Can you guess how? Discuss in the group.

solutions.text: A snake has no legs or feet, but it moves very fast as it slithers over the grass. This is done by alternately contracting and relaxing its body's muscles.
solutions.html: A snake has no legs or feet, but it moves very fast as it slithers over the grass. This is done by alternately contracting and relaxing its body's muscles.

questions.question_text: Can you recall the word used for a cobra's long sharp teeth? Where did you come across this word first? Answer in the context of Garden Snake.
questions.question_html: Can you recall the word used for a cobra’s long sharp teeth? Where did you come across this word first? Answer in the context of Garden Snake.

solutions.text: The word 'fangs' is used for cobra's sharp teeth. They contain poison. So all poisonous snakes have fangs. I first came across this word when my mother asked me to take care while playing in bushy areas, as I may get bitten by a poisonous snake.
solutions.html: The word 'fangs' is used for cobra's sharp teeth. They contain poison. So all poisonous snakes have fangs. I first came across this word when my mother asked me to take care while playing in bushy areas, as I may get bitten by a poisonous snake.
